entry.title: Building Custom Joule Skills via RFC: Guide for SAP S/4HANA and ECC Integration
entry.content:
Introduction

In today’s digital enterprise landscape, rapid access to critical business data can significantly impact operational efficiency and customer satisfaction. SAP’s AI-powered digital assistant, Joule, offers organizations the ability to interact with their enterprise systems through natural language conversations. By extending Joule with custom skills, businesses can create tailored experiences that bring real-time data directly to their teams—without the need for complex navigation through traditional ERP interfaces.

This article provides a comprehensive technical guide for building custom Joule skills that connect to SAP S/4HANA or ECC systems via RFC (Remote Function Call). We’ll explore a practical use case and walk through the complete configuration process.

Business Scenario

Consider a common challenge faced by sales and operations teams: A customer inquires about the manufacturing progress of their order. Traditionally, the specialist would need to log into the ERP system, navigate through multiple screens, and manually locate the production order details. This process is time-consuming and interrupts the flow of customer communication.

With a custom Joule skill, this same specialist can simply ask Joule in natural language—"What’s the status of production order 12345?"—and receive immediate, formatted information directly within SAP Build Workzone. This streamlined approach enhances productivity and improves response times to customer inquiries.

 

 

Solution Architecture

The technical architecture comprises several integrated SAP components:

  • SAP Build Workzone: The user interface where specialists interact with Joule
  • Joule Studio: The development environment for designing and building custom AI skills
  • SAP Build: Used to create Action Projects that expose backend functionality
  • SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP): Hosts destination configurations and orchestrates connections
  • SAP Cloud Connector: Establishes secure RFC connections between BTP and on-premise or private cloud systems
  • SAP S/4HANA / ECC: The backend ERP system containing production order data

 

Configuration Steps

Step 1: Establish RFC Connectivity

The foundation of this solution is a secure connection between your cloud and on-premise environments.

Build RFC Connection with SAP Cloud Connector:
Configure the SAP Cloud Connector to create a secure tunnel between your BTP environment and your S/4HANA or ECC system. This ensures that backend functions can be accessed securely without exposing your entire system to the internet.

 

Setup Destination in BTP Cockpit:
Create a destination in your BTP subaccount that references the RFC connection established through the Cloud Connector. This destination will be consumed by SAP Build to enable communication with your backend system.

 

Step 2: Create a BAPI/RFC Action Project

With connectivity established, the next step is to expose the specific backend functionality that Joule will use.

Create the Action Project:
Navigate to the SAP Build cockpit and create a new BAPI/RFC Action project. Search for the BAPI that retrieves production order information—for example, BAPIs related to production order management.

 

 

 

 

 

 

Test the Connection:
After creating the action project, test the connection to verify that data can be successfully retrieved from your ERP system. By default, these actions operate in READ ONLY mode to protect your business data from unintended modifications.

 

 

Optional Verification with SE37:
For deeper technical validation, you can use transaction code SE37 in your S/4HANA or ECC system to directly test the BAPI function with various parameters. This helps you understand the data structures and responses before integrating them into your Joule skill.

 

 

Step 3: Design Skills in Joule Studio

This is where you design the conversational experience and connect it to your backend data.

Skill 1: Get Production Orders

Each skill consists of three major components:

  1. Skill Description: Define the purpose of the skill and the natural language triggers that will activate it when users interact with Joule.
  2. BAPI Action: Link the skill to the BAPI/RFC Action project you created in SAP Build, establishing the connection between the conversational interface and your backend data.
  3. Send Message: Design the end-user output using the Message Configuration editor. This allows you to transform raw BAPI data into formatted, interactive cards that present information clearly and professionally.

 

 

 

Skill 2: Get Production Order Detail

Following the same pattern, create a second skill that provides detailed information about a specific production order. This allows users to drill down from a list view to detailed information through natural conversation.

 

 

 

Step 4: Deploy and Enable in Workzone

The final phase makes your custom skills accessible to end users.

Create a Shared Environment:
In Joule Studio, create a Shared environment that will serve as the runtime for your deployed skills.

 

 

Deploy Your Joule Project:
Deploy your Joule project to the Shared environment. Once deployed, verify that the project appears under Custom Capabilities.

 

 

Configure Joule in SAP Build Workzone:
Set up the integration between Joule and your SAP Build Workzone site. After completing the integration configuration, enable Joule within your specific Workzone site.

 

 

Test Your Custom Capabilities:
Access your Workzone site, open the Joule client, and test your new custom AI capabilities by asking questions in natural language about production orders.

 

 

Benefits and Outcomes

By implementing custom Joule skills, organizations can achieve:

  • Increased Productivity: Eliminate time-consuming navigation through complex ERP interfaces
  • Improved Customer Service: Enable faster, more accurate responses to customer inquiries
  • Enhanced User Experience: Provide intuitive, conversational access to critical business data
  • Secure Data Access: Maintain enterprise-grade security while democratizing data access
  • Reduced Training Requirements: Lower the learning curve for accessing ERP information

Conclusion

Building custom Joule skills via RFC represents a powerful approach to modernizing how users interact with SAP systems. By combining the conversational AI capabilities of Joule with the deep business logic in S/4HANA or ECC, organizations can create intelligent, context-aware experiences that transform daily operations.

The technical implementation, while requiring careful configuration across multiple components, provides a robust and secure foundation for extending SAP’s AI capabilities to meet specific business needs. As organizations continue their digital transformation journeys, solutions like these demonstrate how AI can be practically applied to solve real business challenges.

Additional Resources

  • Build Your First Joule Skill in Joule Studio
  • Joule Studio CodeJam
  • Build custom Joule skills for SAP and non-SAP systems using Joule Studio

entry.title: VNSG SAP BTP Hackathon 2026: Champions Crowned at SYNC DemoJam
entry.content:
The Stage is Set
The VNSG SAP BTP Hackathon 2026 reached its spectacular grand finale on the main stage of VNSG SYNC 2026 – the annual VNSG and SAP Netherlands year event. In front of a packed keynote audience on 12 March, three finalist teams had exactly five minutes to demonstrate the business impact of their SAP BTP-powered innovations. The audience then cast their votes – and the results were electric.

From Challenge to Champion: The Journey
The hackathon launched in late 2025 when VNSG, SAP BTP Product Management and SAP NL Presales invited Dutch SAP customers and partners to submit real-life business challenges. Six teams were selected and, after a structured enablement phase, spent weeks co-building solutions with dedicated coaches. In February 2026, all six teams pitched to a jury panel, which selected three finalists for the grand DemoJam finale at SYNC. The three finalists stood out for their clear business relevance, technical ambition and the real-world potential to take their prototypes to production.

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

Champion: Promocean + SOA People — Purchase Order Control Center
Luc van Elk and Thomas Langhout from Promocean, supported by SOA People, took the audience vote and claimed the top spot. Their solution – the Purchase Order Control Center – puts SAP Business AI at the heart of procurement operations.
By connecting AI directly to SAP ECC, the team demonstrated how intelligent automation can dramatically reduce manual effort, accelerate purchase order processing, and surface exceptions before they become costly problems. A showcase of how AI on SAP BTP can deliver operational excellence in one of the most common – and pain-prone – business processes in any enterprise.

Runner-up: Ecotone + Expertum — Integration Suite Management
Arjan de Mol and Bart van de Kamp from Ecotone, partnered with Expertum Consulting, earned second place with a solution that tackles a pain many integration teams know all too well: managing and controlling large numbers of iFlows across landscapes. Their Integration Suite Management solution brings Joule-powered automation to iFlow lifecycle management – enabling operators to undeploy and deploy groups of iFlows intelligently. This turns a time-consuming, error-prone manual process into a governed, AI-assisted workflow, giving integration teams transparency, stability and control. 

Third Place: NTT DATA Business Solutions — ProTrack
Laurens Steffers and Jorrit van der Sanden from NTT DATA Business Solutions presented ProTrack – a solution delivering real-time insight for machines on the production floor. Built on SAP BTP, ProTrack addresses a critical operational need in manufacturing: knowing what is happening on your shop floor right now, not minutes later. By streaming machine data into an SAP BTP-powered dashboard, teams can react faster to exceptions, reduce downtime and optimize throughput.

 

Beyond the Podium: A Strong Field
The jury had a hard job. Aarini Consulting, Nederlandse Spoorwegen (NS), and Simac IT & BSC also built
impressive solutions that demonstrated real business value on SAP BTP. Every team that reached the jury pitch proved
that you can go from business challenge to working prototype in a short time - when you combine the right platform
with the right people. A sincere congratulations to all six participating teams !!

Behind the Scenes
Want to experience the atmosphere of the DemoJam from backstage? Watch the behind-the-scenes video - including a
live demo of how SAP Business AI is changing the way people interact with SAP, presented by Jason Enti and Ronald
Schippers: Behind the scenes at the VNSG SAP BTP DemoJam SYNC 2026

The People Who Made it Happen
A hackathon like this does not happen by itself. A huge thank you to everyone who contributed:
Presented at SYNC by: Twan van den Broek and Irene Rompa
Team coaches: Jan Penninkhof , Alex van den Hoek and Twan van den Broek
Jury: Ronald Schippersm Steven Nuyts, Steffen Schad, Jonathan Freeland
Made possible by: Mientje Paais, Olaf Winkler, Judy Bui and Niklas Siemer

What This Proves
The VNSG SAP BTP Hackathon 2026 is proof that innovation does not require a blank slate or a greenfield project. It requires the right platform, the right people and a real problem worth solving.
SAP BTP – combining SAP Integration Suite, SAP Build, SAP HANA Cloud, SAP Business AI and cloud-native runtimes – gives teams everything they need to move from prototype to production at speed.
The finalist solutions were not demos for the sake of demos. They addressed real procurement pain, real integration
complexity and real manufacturing visibility gaps. And they were built in weeks, not months.

What's Next?
The conversation does not end on the SYNC stage. All teams are encouraged to work with their coaches to plan production rollouts and turn hackathon prototypes into enterprise solutions.

For the broader SAP Community : Stay tuned for the next edition of the VNSG SAP BTP Hackathon. Bring your own
real-life business challenge and co-build with partners and SAP experts.
And who knows - your solution could be on the SYNC main stage in 2027.

SAP Business Technology Platform SAP Build SAP Integration Suite SAP Business AI 

@hansvp @qmrjvd @sricsi98 @BvE @mdschoenmakers @tamasszirtes @f_van_leeuwen @Petra1 @BartvdKamp @tedcastelijns @Arjan_deMol @dvvelzen @JdTeuling @AnnikaHeus @LaurensSteffers

Related reading:
When Innovation Meets Real Business Impact – VNSG SAP BTP Hackathon 2026
Grand Finale VNSG SAP BTP Hackathon in a DemoJam

entry.title: Joule for Developers ABAP AI Sapphire and ASUG 2026 Sessions
entry.content:
In case you do not have planned your Sapphire Orlando and ASUG agenda here are my recommendations if you want to learn about the latest innovations from ABAP Platform and Joule for developers (J4D) ABAP AI capabilities:

 

  1. ABAP Cloud: Entering the new era of ABAP development | BTP2107: Sonja Lienard (Head of ABAP Platform, SAP SE) will explain ABAP Cloud development model, agentic development experience in VS Code, the SAP Joule for Developers add-on, and agentic custom code migration and the strategy behind the evolution.
  2. Reimagine transformation with migration and modernization assistants | ERP2493: Sonja Lienard (Head of ABAP Platform, SAP SE) and Sören Ruder (Chief Product Officer and Global Head of Customer Experience & Solutions) will explore how SAP agentic AI will accelerate ERP migration and modernization by compressing timelines, and unlock faster time-to-value on your journey to a modern, cloud ERP.
  3. Introduction to ABAP Cloud supported by generative AI | BTP1429: Get an overview on ABAP Cloud and recent innovations, get insights on Clean Core and see how generative AI boosts your adoption of ABAP Cloud conducted by Volker Drees and Nora Klemp (ABAP Platform Product Management).
  4. Build smarter: Agentic ABAP development tools for Visual Studio Code | BTP2573: Simona Marincei (Head of AI - SAP ABAP Platform) and Holger Bruchelt (Product Manager, Microsoft Corporation) will show how ABAP agents orchestrate in VS Code via the ABAP MCP-server tasks, speed up coding, and boost productivity across orchestrators from various partners.
  5. Agentic ABAP with SAP Joule for Developers: Build faster, migrate smarter | JOU1428: Simona Marincei (Head of AI - SAP ABAP Platform), Holger Bruchelt (Product Manager, Microsoft Corporation) and Nora Klemp (Senior Product Specialist) will explain how agentic ABAP accelerates SAP S/4HANA migrations and streamline ABAP Cloud development across modern and legacy environments.
  6. Customer Influence and feedback: The product road map for ABAP Cloud | BTP1603: Learn from Vanessa Micelli-Schmidt (VP, Head of ABAP Platform Experience, SAP SE) how to give feedback to the ABAP product roadmap with the Customer Influence site which allows submitting, tracking, and prioritizing ideas.
  7. Modernize classic extensions for clean core in SAP Cloud ERP Private | BTP1426: Volker Drees (Product Expert, ABAP Cloud, SAP SE) will explain clean core extensibility practice in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition. Learn how to govern clean core development using the ABAP test cockpit and how to use the ABAP Cloud development model and clean core extensibility principles to modernize your custom on-stack extensions.

 

The full Sapphire Orlando and ASUG 2026 (11.05.2026 - 13.05.2026) agenda can be found here...
...and here is the agenda for Sapphire Madrid (19.05.2026 - 21.05.26).
 
Here you can learn more:
  1. Our Roadmap for 2026 before Sapphire
  2. Your Roadmap for getting started with ABAP AI
  3. ABAP AI Revolution Accelerates: The ABAP Developer Who Built Enterprise Apps in Minutes
  4. Joule for Developers and ABAP AI capabilities are coming to SAP S/4HANA Private Edition 2021, 2022, and 2023
  5. Public Roadmap: SAP Help / SAP Roadmap Explorer
  6. Help & Guides
    1. Step-by-step guide how to activate SAP Joule for Developers, ABAP AI capabilities for your SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition
    2. Joule for Developers (J4D) for S/4HANA Public Cloud Setup Guide
    3. SAP ABAP AI Discovery Center
    4. SAP BTP Discovery Center (Free Tier Services)
    5. Custom code migration to SAP S/4HANA powered by SAP Joule for Developers, ABAP AI capabilities
    6. YouTube playlist for Joule for Developers (J4D)
    7. Joule for Developers Help (J4D)
    8. Build SAP Fiori Apps with ABAP Cloud and SAP Joule for developers (RAP120) 
    9. ADT Eclipse AI Capabilities Help
    10. ABAP AI SDK Help
    11. ABAP-1 Help

entry.title: What's New in SAP BTP - Q1 2026 Innobytes
entry.content:
This first quarter has set the tone for 2026, bringing in new ideas and innovations all around Data, Joule and more. Let’s dive into some of the highlights Q1 has brought us.

 

 

AI & Data Foundation Highlight

Discovery and Data Agent in SAP HANA Cloud

The new Discovery and Data Agents are lowering learning barriers for developers and data users who just need answers. These new agents make it easier to explore, analyze, and act on data without needing deep knowledge of the underlying artifacts.

To see this demo in action, watch the video (starting at 0:11).

Learn more

  • Discovery & Data Agent on SAP Community
  • Register for the SAP HANA Cloud Data & Discovery Agents - Beta Program

 

Integration Highlight

Fresh Connections in SAP Integration Suite

New adapters for SAP Integration Suite on SAP BTP help transform your integration landscape, breaking down data silos, streamlining operations, and allow rapid adoption of new capabilities to innovate faster. 

The latest release offers:

  • The BambooHR adapter enables receiving and processing employee lifecycle data effectively.
  • The Microsoft Outlook Sender and Receiver adapter allows seamless integration for calendars, mail, attachments, and messaging directly into your mission-critical workflows. 

For Edge Integration Cella:

  • The File adapter, enabling secure file exchange with NFS systems
  • The OFTP2 adapter for compliant B2B connections to securely exchange messages from business partners. 

See the new integration content (starting at 2:14).

Learn more:

  • Integration Adapter
  • SAP Integration Suite on SAP Community

 

Agent & Application Development Highlights

API based Action Error Handling

In real-world automation, failures are inevitable. What matters is how the system handles them. This innovation brings precision and predictability to API error handling in SAP Build Process Automation on SAP BTP. When configuring an API-based Action step, enable error handling and define specific errors, handling authentication failures precisely. You can create generic categories, such as 4XX or 5XX, handling broader client or server issues.

The inability to define category overlap eliminates ambiguity, and deterministic runtime behavior is guaranteed. When an error occurs, precedence is strictly enforced:

  • Specific errors first
  • Generic category second
  • Undefined fallback last

At the Action level, you can configure retries that define the number of attempts and the delay between them. Afterwards, the error handling logic is executed. Every stage is fully logged in Monitoring, ensuring complete traceability and turning error handling from a black box into a controlled, observable workflow decision.

Watch the demo (starting at 3:35).

 

Conversational Work in SAP Build Work Zone 

There is now a powerful new way to work in SAP Build Work Zone. Save time by simply asking Joule what you need instead of clicking through multiple pages.

In the demo (starting at 5:33), a new project workspace for a cross-functional initiative is set up through a short conversation with Joule, enabling easy navigation, no technical steps, and only one screen. SAP Build Work Zone becomes more conversational, more intuitive, and more aligned with how people actually work.

Learn more:

  • Joule in SAP Build Work Zone

 

Core Platform Highlight

SAP Automation Pilot and Joule: Unlocking AI-Powered Ops Agents

The SAP Automation Pilot and Joule turn repeatable operational tasks into intelligent, agent-driven capabilities on SAP BTP.

By starting with a real operational process like a S/4HANA health check, use Automation Pilot’s low-code/no-code interface. Here, the necessary steps, inputs and validations can be collected and grouped into a Model Context Protocol (MCP) Server. The MCP Server exposes these automations as governed, reusable tools that can be safely consumed by AI agents without writing custom APIs.

Once published, connect it to Joule Studio to build a Joule Ops Agent that understands when and how to invoke these tools. The Ops Agent can now detect issues or fetch system status, triggering the right automation behind the scenes.

The DevOps or Admin team can simply ask the Ops Agent in Joule to ‘check my HANA Cloud and App instances health and remediate common issues’ by using the MCP Server to execute these steps securely, consistently, and audibly.

For a demo, watch the video (starting at 7:35).

Learn more:

  • Blog Post: From Automation to Agentic Ops
  • Blog Post: Meet your Ops Agents

 

Share your thoughts 

These videos are for YOU, and we would love to hear your impressions. How do you like the video? How can we improve upcoming videos? Please share your feedback by liking this post or leaving a comment below.

Thank you for your support and happy viewing, sharing, and enjoying!

You Might Also Be Interested In

  • SAP BTP Garage: monthly LIVE webinar with hands-on use case walkthroughs and demos.
  • Unlocking SAP BTP: monthly podcast covering all things SAP BTP.

entry.title: SAP HANA Cloud: Expert-Guided Implementation Workshop Series
entry.content:
SAP HANA Cloud is a fully managed, in-memory cloud database service as part of the SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP). It delivers high-speed processing and real-time analytics, which is especially valuable for data-intensive applications. The platform supports multiple data models, including relational, graph, spatial, JSON, and document store, allowing developers to work with different types of data in one environment. It also includes built-in data virtualization and integration capabilities, enabling organizations to access and combine data from SAP and non-SAP sources.

SAP HANA Cloud provides a comprehensive environment for building intelligent, data-driven solutions. It supports advanced analytics, machine learning, and predictive capabilities directly within the database, which reduces data movement and accelerates insight generation. Developers can use familiar tools and languages such as SQL, Java, Node.js, and Python to create applications and services.
The platform also includes data lake functionality for storing and processing large volumes of less-frequently accessed data at a lower cost, while still allowing seamless querying alongside in-memory data. Its multi-tier storage approach helps organizations balance performance and cost by automatically managing where data resides. Overall, SAP HANA Cloud enables organizations to modernize their data landscape, support real-time decision-making, and drive digital transformation initiatives.

 

🚀 Empower Your Data Journey: Introducing Our New SAP HANA Cloud Expert Guided Implementation Series 

In today’s fast-paced digital landscape, speed of transformation is a must. Businesses across the globe are evolving to make faster, smarter, and more connected decisions by building intelligent, data-driven solutions using advanced analytics, machine learning, and predictive capabilities. To support this evolution, we’re excited to announce our renewed series of Expert-Guided Implementation workshops (EGI) from SAP Enterprise Support designed to help you upskill, modernize, and future-proof your business processes. Whether you're looking to modernize legacy systems, harness the power of cloud data, or unlock real-time insights through analytics, this workshop series offers targeted enablement for every stage of your development. 

*The following EGI sessions will run throughout the year, with quarterly updates to the schedule. 
Each session is limited to 30 individual customer participants.  
**Registration links below direct to SAP Learning content for SAP Enterprise Support, which requires a one-time registration with a valid S-user.

Configuration, Administration, Security and Monitoring topics for SAP HANA Cloud

In this live EGI session we will guide you through the SAP HANA Cloud capabilities as DBaaS, demonstrate the key features available in the latest quarterly release of the tool and show the benefits of using the administration tools for DB Monitoring, security and configuration. For details, please read the abstract here.

Agenda: 

  • Introduction to SAP HANA Cloud
  • Overview of SAP BTP
  • Introduction and usage of Administration tools
    • SAP HANA Cloud Central
    • SAP HANA Cockpit
    • SAP HANA Database Explorer
  • Managing, Monitoring and Alert Notification in SAP HANA Cloud
  • Workload Management:
    • Managing Tables
    • HANA NSE
  • SAP HANA HDI Overview
  • Introduction and usage of Cloud Foundry CLI 
  • Security Administration and User management

Register Here* 

 

SAP HANA Cloud Modeling

In this live EGI session, we will guide you through the process of modeling in SAP HANA Cloud. After the completion of the session, you will have the knowledge of calculation view usage and design in SAP HANA Cloud utilizing the SAP Business Application studio. Also, we showcase the core capabilities of SAP HANA Cloud for calculation view graphical modelling and provide help on about SQL Script procedures and implementing functions using the SAP Cloud Application Programming Model (CAP). For details, please read the abstract here.

Agenda: 

  • Introduction to SAP BTP and SAP HANA Cloud
  • Usage of SAP Business Application Studio and Administration tools
  • Deploy SAP Business Application Studio
  • Introduction and modeling Calculation views
  •  Modeling with SAP Business Application Studio and using Database Explorer
  • Modeling features
  • Generating restricted and calculated columns
  • SQL Script usage
  • Performance Analysis, Debug mode & SQL Analyser
  • Overview of Best Practices

 Register Here* 

 

SAP HANA Cloud Multiple Model Programming including Vector Engine and Knowledge Graph

In this live EGI session, we empower customers with the knowledge of SAP HANA Cloud multiple model programming using the latest concepts including vector engine, knowledge graph engine and retrieval augmented generation. After the completion of the workshop, customers will have the knowledge of multiple model engines and how to incorporate artificial intelligence and retrieval augmented generation capabilities.

Agenda: 

  • Introduction to SAP BTP and SAP HANA Cloud
  • Understand SAP HANA Cloud architecture and deployment options
  • Introduction to multiple model programming
  • Data Tiering considerations for multiple model programming
  • Overview of SAP Business Application Studio
  • Spatial data concepts in SAP HANA Cloud
  • Understanding spatial features and functions
  • Spatial data modeling and processing
  • Graph processing in SAP HANA Cloud
  • JSON document store in SAP HANA Cloud
  • Automated machine learning (AutoML) in SAP HANA Cloud
  • SAP HANA Cloud vector database
  • Capabilities and benefits of multiple model processing with vectors
  • SAP HANA Cloud knowledge graph engine
  • Knowledge graph and Retrieval Augmented Generation (RAG)
  • Real-world applications for Retrieval Augmented Generation

Register Here* 

 

Pro-code cloud application programming development through SAP Build and AI (Joule)

In this live EGI session we will empower you with the knowledge of cloud application programming development in SAP HANA Cloud. After the completion of the session, you will have the knowledge of CAP CDS development and design in SAP HANA Cloud and understand how to build and deploying cloud application projects from scratch and with reduced costs utilizing pro-code development using SAP Build Code with Joule. For details, please read the abstract here.

Agenda:

  • Introduction to SAP BTP and SAP HANA Cloud
  • Introduction to SAP Build Code and Prompt Engineering
  • CAP Architecture and SAP Business Application Studio features
  • Build and deploy a CAP project using SAP Business Application Studio
  • Modeling in SAP HANA Cloud using CAP CDS
  • Modeling in SAP HANA Cloud Optimization
  • Building Application using SAP Build Code and Joule
  • Modeling in SAP HANA Cloud Management and Administration

Register Here* 

Learning Outcomes from the EGI Series:

  • Configuration, Administration, Security and Monitoring topics for SAP HANA Cloud:
    Master SAP HANA Cloud configuration, performance monitoring and secure user management using standard administration tools.
  • SAP HANA Cloud Modeling:
    Develop Calculation views and SQL Script procedures within SAP Business Application Studio.
  • SAP HANA Cloud Multiple Model Programming including Vector Engine and Knowledge Graph:
    Implement advanced Vector Engine, Knowledge Graph, and Spatial Data capabilities to power Generative AI and Retrieval Augmented Generation scenarios
  • Pro-code cloud application programming development through SAP Build and AI (Joule):
    Build and deploy end-to-end CAP applications efficiently using SAP Build Code and Joule


Who Should Attend? 

This workshop series is ideal for developers, modelers, business analysts, system administrators, database administrators and SAP customers looking to transform their business processes. Whether you're just getting started or want to go deeper into SAP's modern toolset, there’s a session tailored for your needs. 

 

GET MORE CONTENT LIKE THIS!

 SAP Enterprise Support Value Maps — your go-to team for Foundational Success Plan support and empowerment — have a dedicated SAP Community Group.

As part of your SAP cloud subscription, you get access to:

  • Expert-led resources
  • Tailored recommendations
  • Advisory services
  • …all designed to help you accelerate innovation and success.


👉 Visit our Community Group
Sign in with your S-user ID or Universal ID, then hit “Subscribe” to stay updated.

Looking to dive deeper into data-driven innovation?
Within the group, explore the Data & Analytics Value Map to connect with your Foundational Support team. Simply click “Join Group”, and once inside, you can even request a call with a topic expert to get personalized guidance and enablement support.

entry.title: Migration tool for your SAP Build Apps frontend web applications
entry.content:
In my former blog post, I announced that SAP Build Apps will be deprecated, and today I’m happy to share that a migration tool for your frontend web application built with SAP Build Apps is ready to support you with your transition needs. Previously, when you wanted to transition from SAP Build Apps frontend web application offering to SAP Build tools, you hit a brick wall. You were left with a proprietary .mtar export file and a harsh realization: migrating meant essentially rebuilding your entire application from scratch.

Over the past period, we’ve received a tremendous amount of great, honest, and passionate feedback from our customers, partners, and community. Our proposed migration path of the backend is working, but the recreation of the web application frontend was not acceptable. And this is why we focused all our efforts on creating a transformer tool to support your migration needs. We believe in developer empowerment and digital freedom. Your data, your logic, and your UI designs belong to you. Your software should scale with your ambition, not be constrained by the platform you started on. With SAP Build, you have the right choice.

Today, I am incredibly proud to introduce a migration tool to transform your .mtar file of your frontend web apps into React or SAP UI5. This transformation tool is an automated engine designed to do the heavy lifting of application migration. It takes your raw, exported .mtar archives, securely processes the proprietary configurations, and intelligently translates them into clean and standardized frontend projects which you can easily enhance leveraging your SAP Build environment like SAP Business Application Studio.
Instead of spending weeks manually reverse-engineering views, layouts, and navigation logic, the transformation tool delivers a fully structured React (Vite) or SAP UI5 project.

Starting today, customers of our SAP Build Apps standard offering which are affected by the deprecation can reach out to us directly with the ask to migrate their SAP Build Apps frontend web app into either React or SAP UI5. The process is described here. With React you also have the chance to leverage the SAP Sales & Service Cloud V2 design system (colors, typography, BEM components). Your SAP contact partner is also equipped and can help you navigate if you are currently on an implementation project. Certainly, partners can also reach out to us to realize their clients’ projects.

 

Let’s Build the Future Together

To all the developers, architects, and IT leaders who shared their frustrations and their visions with us: Thank you! Your feedback was the catalyst for this innovation. The transformer isn’t just a conversion tool, it’s our commitment to supporting your growth at every stage of the software lifecycle.

We can't wait to see what you build next!

And finally, stay tuned! SAPPHIRE is around the corner!

 

Update (2026-04-14):

To provide the relevant .mtar file as described on help.sap.com please go into the lobby. Within the lobby choose your project. Afterwards, choose "Versions". Then the 3 horizontal dots behind the relevant version and choose "Export". This downloads the .mtar file into your download folder. In case you are facing issues uploading an .mtar into the ticket. Just pack it as a .zip and upload it.

entry.title: From SAP Build Apps to Pro-Code SAPUI5 Vibe Coded with UI5 MCP Server
entry.content:
Context

 
If you've been building frontends with SAP Build Apps, you've probably already seen the announcement. Starting March 23, 2026, SAP Build Apps transitions into the unified SAP Build platform, a broader, more integrated offering. More info. "As part of the introduction of the unified SAP Build offering in April 2025, our goal has been to simplify the experience and bring together all customer needs into a single, integrated platform. As we continue to evolve and streamline the SAP Build portfolio to meet the evolving market needs and AI trends, SAP Build Apps will be deprecated as a standalone product starting March 23, 2026."

The migration guidance for existing projects recommends a fresh start: export your data as `.csv` files and rebuild in a CAP-based SAP Build project. For the data layer, that's straightforward. But what about the frontend? That's where things get interesting, and where my story begins, because I am having some projects that I have to migrate. I started with a simple one. So, here is my story with my first project. It is an easy one, but it establishes what can be my future migrations, so I want to share this with you.

 

What I had: an order approval form built in SAP Build Apps

 
The original app was a simple Order Creation form. Users would fill in four fields: Customer, Material, Amount, and Delivery Date, then hit a "Get Approval" button, and a SAP Build Process Automation (BPA) workflow would kick off in the background.

It worked. And honestly, for a no-code/low-code form, it looks exactly like what it is, a canvas-built UI that got the job done fast:
 
With the platform evolution underway, this was the perfect moment to explore what the same app would look like rebuilt in pro-code SAPUI5.

 

The opportunity: a clean slate for pro-code

 
SAP's migration guidance is intentionally manual, export CSVs, rebuild in CAP. For the backend data, that's straightforward. For the frontend, it's a blank canvas.
The question I asked myself: Can I use AI tooling to recreate this frontend in pro-code SAPUI5, staying true to the original UX, without spending days writing boilerplate?
Spoiler: yes. Here's how I managed it.

 

Step 1: Document the existing app with some browser skills.

 
In my case, I did not have any documentation around this frontend. So, before writing a single line of code, I used an MCP which can see what is going on in my browser directly inside the coding agent to inspect the live SAP Build Apps frontend. This meant I could:
  1. Navigate to the running app programmatically
  2. Take screenshots
  3. Capture the full accessibility tree (all labels, inputs, button text, page structure)
  4. Inspect network requests to understand the BPA API call being made
The MCP server gave the coding agent "eyes" on the browser. Instead of me manually describing the UI, the coding agent could see it. The field labels, the placeholder text, the button label, even the navigation pages. This became the spec for the new app.

 

Step 2: Vibe-code the SAPUI5 app with the UI5 MCP server

 
With the original UI fully documented (it is important that in every step of the discovery steps, the agent documents it), I used the SAPUI5 MCP server to scaffold and build a proper freestyle SAPUI5 application.
The UI5 MCP server knows:
  1. The full SAPUI5 API reference
  2. How to validate manifests
  3. How to run the UI5 linter
  4. SAPUI5 best practices and guidelines
Instead of manually looking up every sap.m control or fighting with manifest.json structure, the coding agent could ask the MCP server directly: "What's the right way to set up an MCP-compliant ColumnLayout form?", and get a precise, version-aware answer.
The result was a full freestyle SAPUI5 project.
 

Step 3: Wire up SAP Build Process Automation

 
The BPA integration was the trickiest part to get right. The original Build Apps frontend was triggering a workflow, via a REST call to /workflow/rest/v1/workflow-instances.

In the pro-code app, this was handled by:
  1. A BTP Destination pointing at the BPA API endpoint
  2. An xs-app.json route that proxies requests through that destination
  3. A plain fetch() call in the controller, no special SDK needed.
The controller also handles the CSRF token pattern properly and uses `MessageBox` to give the user feedback on success or failure.
 
In this case, I needed to use a couple of prompts, but then it was working smoothly. So this lead to a new frontend with same workflow. Same payload shape. Different frontend.
 

Step 4: Deploy to BTP Cloud Foundry

 

The app was packaged as a standard Multi-Target Application (MTA):

  • An mta.yaml that defines the CF modules: the HTML5 app, XSUAA service binding, HTML5 Application Repository, and the destination service...
  • A xs-security.json XSUAA security descriptor
  • A xs-app.json app router config with the route to proxy.

One mbt build + cf deploy later, it was live in the same BTP subaccount, accessible from the SAP BTP Launchpad.
 

Result

 

Here's the new app:
 

Same four fields. Same "Get Approval" button. Same BPA workflow trigger. But now:
  • It's proper SAPUI5
  • It follows Fiori UX guidelines
  • It's maintainable
  • It's future-proof

Tools used

 
The thing that made this feel like "vibe coding" rather than a grinding migration project was the combination of two MCP servers working together inside the coding agent:
  • Browser MCP: Gave the coding agent a live view of the old app, screenshots, DOM, accessibility tree, network calls
  • SAPUI5 MCP Server: Gave the coding agent precise, version-aware SAPUI5 API knowledge and best-practice validation
Without the browser MCP, I would have had to manually describe every field, label, and interaction to the coding agent. With it, the coding agent could see the app and extract everything automatically.
Without the UI5 MCP server, the coding agent would be working from training data that may be out of date with the latest SAPUI5 version. With it, every control, property, and pattern was looked up against the real API reference.
Together, they turned what could have been a multi-day migration into a focused, AI-assisted session.
 

Some thoughts

No automatic migration doesn't mean no help. AI tooling with the right MCP servers can dramatically compress the effort of rebuilding a UI from scratch.

Also, I would really recommend to document before you rebuild. Using the proper tools to fully inspect the live app before writing any code meant the new app faithfully replicated the original UX, no guessing. (Also, if in your case you have documentation around you frontend apps, use them for enhancing the vibe coding experience).

Another feeling that I have is that: vibe coding is real. when the AI has the right tools (MCP servers with domain knowledge), you can describe what you want and let it figure out how. The code quality is solid because the AI is working from authoritative sources, not hallucinating APIs.

It is worth to clarify that this method that I have used is not a silver bullet for all the projects (and this one was a straight forward one). And depending on the complexity of the same, some other skills, MCPs, agents... can be used. But in this case in which I had to transform this UI, this procedure was really useful for me. In a couple of hours I was able to deliver this and to document it. So, from my point of view, it is worth the try.

 
The current app is a faithful port of the original. But now that it's in pro-code, the sky's the limit.
The platform evolution turned out to be a great excuse to invest in a robust, maintainable, and extensible stack and with MCP-powered AI assistance.

entry.title: From SAP Build Apps to SAP Build: Rethinking Low-Code Development in the SAP Ecosystem
entry.content:
SAP Build Apps has its roots in SAP’s acquisition of AppGyver, a powerful no-code platform that enabled rapid application development without traditional coding.

What SAP Build Apps Enabled

  • Drag-and-drop UI creation – This is much appreciated by the business
  • Visual logic flows – Good to visualize compared to syntax of the code
  • Mobile-first app design – It matters because 60% of the traffic is from mobile devices
  • Citizen developer empowerment – You have an idea – Voila – then start building!

It played a key role in:

  • Democratizing application development as the business users can build if it is a simple use case
  • Accelerating prototyping – Accessibility accelerated prototyping and check
  • Enabling business-led innovation – democratizing the application development & prototype acceleration enabled the innovation that can be business led.

SAP Build Apps followed a flat architecture, where UI and logic were tightly coupled, making it ideal for simple applications but limiting for enterprise-scale solutions. In the current scenario of ecosystem, while simplicity to build the apps helps but it hinders scale or prevents from building complex enterprise-scale solutions.

And here comes the ‘sunset of SAP Build Apps’ as a standalone strategic tool and it is not a step backward — it is a strategic evolution.

Key Drivers Behind the Transition

  1. From UI-Centric to Process-Centric Development

Organizations today require:

  • Workflow-driven applications rather than UI centric. The UI should be really in alignment with the workflow.
  • Automation-first solutions

SAP Build Apps was primarily: UI-focused, not process-driven

  1. Need for Workflow Orchestration

Modern enterprise apps demand:

  • Approval workflows – If processes are waiting for approvals, then the delay/wait time is inducing lag in creation of value to the business.
  • Task routing – We do not need manual intervention to route the tasks the orchestration should happen automatic for non-critical routing of tasks.
  • Process monitoring – Command center set up has become important for the businesses so that process monitoring can be done to understand the non-value adding sub-processes.

These capabilities require a dedicated process layer, which was not native to SAP Build Apps.

  1. Integration as a First-Class Requirement

Applications must seamlessly connect with:

  • SAP S/4HANA
  • APIs
  • External ecosystems

SAP Build Apps had limited enterprise integration capabilities.

  1. Unified Development Experience

SAP’s strategic direction is to unify:

  • Low-code
  • Pro-code
  • Automation
  • AI

into a single platform.

Enter SAP Build: The Unified Platform

SAP Build represents SAP’s next-generation development environment.

 What SAP Build Brings Together in summary -

Capability Description

App Development

Build responsive UI applications

Process Automation

Workflow, approvals, business rules

Workspaces

Business sites and launchpads

Pro-Code Extensions

CAP, APIs, advanced logic

AI Integration

Joule-powered assistance

SAP Build introduces a layered architecture, separating UI, process, and backend — enabling scalability and maintainability.

Use Case: Let us take one example and understand better.

Purchase Requisition is the most common Application

Let’s examine a simple yet powerful scenario:

Business Flow:

  1. Employee creates a purchase request
  2. Manager reviews the request
  3. Approval or rejection decision
  4. Status tracking

SAP Build Apps vs SAP Build: A Practical Comparison

 Architecture & Capability Comparison

Criteria SAP Build Apps SAP Build

Parameter

SAP Build Apps

SAP Build

Architecture

Flat (UI + logic combined)

Layered (UI + Workflow + Services)

Development Speed

Very fast

Moderate

Ease of Use

High (citizen developer friendly)

Medium

Workflow Capability

Manual logic

Built-in workflow engine

Process Handling

Simulated

Real orchestration

Integration

Limited

Strong SAP + API integration

Scalability

Low

Enterprise-grade

Maintainability

Complex for large apps

Modular and structured

Extensibility

Limited

Pro-code ready

AI Capabilities

Minimal

Integrated AI features

Future Readiness

Deprecated

Strategic platform

Deep Dive: Application Behavior

 SAP Build Apps Approach

  • Purchase Request is created via UI
  • Status of the purchase request is updated manually
  • Logic embedded within the app

Limitation:
No real workflow, no auditability, no scalability

SAP Build Approach

  • Request triggers workflow
  • Manager receives approval task
  • Decision updates system automatically

Advantage:
Process-driven, auditable, scalable, enterprise-ready

Conclusion

SAP Build Apps was instrumental in enabling rapid innovation and empowering citizen developers.

However, as enterprise requirements evolved, SAP Build emerged as the strategic platform, offering:

  • End-to-end process orchestration
  • Seamless integration
  • Scalable architecture
  • AI-powered development

The future is not just about building apps —
It is about building intelligent, integrated business solutions.

entry.title: Transforming Manual Sales Order Processing into an Intelligent SAP BTP Workflow
entry.content:
Many organizations still rely on manual, email-driven sales order processing. Business users receive customer documents, extract key details, validate information, and create orders in SAP S/4HANA. While manageable at low volumes, this approach becomes time-consuming, error-prone, and difficult to scale as document complexity and volume increase.

Reimagining the Process with SAP BTP

This solution transforms the traditional process into an intelligent, end-to-end workflow using:

  • SAP Build Process Automation for orchestration
  • SAP Document AI for document understanding
  • SAP CAP (Cloud Application Programming Model) for validation, enrichment, and user interaction

The result is a centralized, application-driven experience that minimizes manual effort while maintaining business control.

Solution Overview

The automated workflow replaces fragmented manual steps with a streamlined process:

  1. Incoming emails are monitored via SAP Build Process Automation
  2. Attachments are sent to SAP Document AI for data extraction
  3. Document AI extracts relevant sales order fields using a custom schema
  4. A CAP application retrieves and processes the extracted data
  5. Additional validation and enrichment is performed (e.g., master data checks, missing fields, amount validation)
  6. Business users review and approve data in a centralized interface before posting to SAP S/4HANA

Architecture Highlights

The solution is built on SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP):

  • SAP Build Process Automation → event-driven orchestration
  • SAP Document AI → document extraction
  • SAP CAP → business logic, validation, enrichment, UI
  • SAP S/4HANA → system of record
  • Connectivity & Destinations → secure integration via APIs

This architecture enables scalable automation with human-in-the-loop control.

Why This Approach Matters

Document extraction alone is not enough. True automation requires business context, validation, and enrichment.

The CAP layer bridges this gap by:

  • Combining extracted data with SAP master data
  • Applying business rules and validations
  • Providing a controlled user review interface

This ensures accuracy while reducing manual effort.

Key Advantages

  • Reduced manual handoffs
  • Improved validation and enrichment control
  • Enhanced user experience through a centralized interface
  • Seamless integration with SAP S/4HANA
  • Scalable and future-ready architecture

Business Impact

This solution addresses the core challenge of converting unstructured documents into structured SAP transactions.

Customer results include:

  • ~30% increase in order-processing capacity
  • ~90% reduction in manual effort per transaction
  • Improved accuracy and reduced rework
  • Faster month-end and quarter-end close
  • Cleaner audit trail and compliance

Conclusion

By consolidating extraction, validation, enrichment, and user review into a single workflow, organizations can move from manual processing to intelligent automation - improving efficiency while maintaining control.

If you have any further questions, please leave a comment below or send your inquiries to the email CoE_AI_PlanningSpendExec@global.corp.sap. We will be happy to get back to you.

entry.title: CSV Validator App Is Now Open Source
entry.content:
From Solution to Community Tool

A few weeks ago, I shared how CSVValidationApp helps solve data quality issues in SAP SuccessFactors Talent Intelligence Hub. The response surprised me. Many have built similar workarounds, spent hours cleaning data manually, or watched perfectly good imports fail because of stray HTML tags.

That's when I felt this shouldn't be "my tool." It should be our tool.

Today, CSV Validator App is officially open source and available on GitHub under the Apache 2.0 license: github.com/SAP/csv-validator

Why This Matters

I didn't build this tool because I love CSV files (I don't). I built it because I was tired of seeing good work derailed by preventable issues. An HR team prepares a skills library update, exports clean data, imports it into TIH—and suddenly descriptions are broken, special characters are corrupted, and they're troubleshooting instead of enabling their workforce.

It's frustrating. And it's common.

Making this open source isn't about recognition or downloads. It's about honesty: I don't have all the answers. You might be importing recruiting data with different quirks. You might support a region with specific character encoding needs. You might have found a validation rule I missed.

By opening the code, we can solve these problems together—transparently, collaboratively, and faster than any of us could alone.

What You're Getting

This is a complete SAP Fiori application you can run today:

Core Validation:

  • Detects encoding issues (UTF-8 compliance, BOM, null bytes)
  • Validates CSV structure (headers, delimiters, column consistency)
  • Identifies problematic content (HTML tags, entities, control characters, special characters)
  • Checks field lengths and data integrity

Auto-Correction:

  • Strips HTML tags while preserving content
  • Converts HTML entities to UTF-8 characters (  → space, ü → ü)
  • Properly escapes special characters
  • Generates a clean, download-ready corrected file

UI Features:

  • Drag-and-drop file upload
  • Interactive preview of your CSV
  • Filterable table showing issues by row and type
  • Side-by-side view of original vs. corrected content
  • One-click download of the corrected file

Privacy:

  • Everything runs locally in your browser
  • No uploads, no servers, no data leaves your machine

It's built with SAP UI5, follows SAP Fiori standards, and runs via npm. Five minutes from clone to validation.

Getting Started

git clone https://github.com/SAP/csv-validator.git
cd csv-validator
npm install
npm start

Open your browser to the app url, drop in a CSV file, and click Validate. That's it.
You can test by uploading your csv and press validate. Here is a sample screenshot of what you can expect:

 

Where We Go From Here

I have ideas for improvements—support for other SuccessFactors modules, batch processing, direct API integration. But I'm more interested in hearing yours.

If you use this tool and it helps, tell me what's missing. If it doesn't catch an issue it should, open a GitHub issue with an example. If you have a fix or enhancement, submit a pull request. If you just want to share your experience, leave a comment below.

This isn't a finished product. It's a starting point. I'm learning as much from your use cases as you might learn from the code.

An Honest Ask

If you work with SuccessFactors data imports—whether you're an administrator, consultant, or developer—try this tool with your real files. Not a sanitized demo file. Your actual data (with sensitive info removed, of course).

Then tell me:

  • Did it catch issues you would have missed?
  • Did it flag false positives that aren't actually problems?
  • What validation rule would make your life easier?

And if you've built something similar or have ideas for collaboration, let's talk. The goal isn't to centralize everything into this one tool—it's to raise the baseline for data quality across the community.

Resources:

  • GitHub Repository: github.com/SAP/csv-validator
  • Original Blog Post: Bridging Data Quality Gaps in TIH with CSVValidationApp
  • Issues & Feedback: github.com/SAP/csv-validator/issues
  • Contribution Guidelines: github.com/SAP/csv-validator/CONTRIBUTING.md

Data quality shouldn't be a bottleneck. Let's make it a strength—together.

entry.title: Integration capabilities highly rated in SAP BTP reviews
entry.content:
Only a few short months ago I posted a blog in celebration of SAP Business Technology Platform's (SAP BTP) performance in G2’s Winter 2026 awards for their Cloud Platform as a Service (PaaS) category. Now having quickly accelerated to their Spring 2026 awards, once again I am happy to report that SAP BTP featured prominently, achieving leader badges along with a leaders position in the G2 Enterprise Grid for CPaaS.  

 Connections count!

Reading just a handful of the most recent SAP BTP reviews on the G2 website reveals just how important robust yet flexible integration is to SAP customers. Among the most recent customer reviews, many reveal Integration as something for which they rate SAP BTP highly:

  • 18 April 2026 I use SAP Business Technology Platform for data integration, analytics, and app development. It connects multiple systems, reduces data silos, and streamlines workflows, providing real-time insights. Its scalability handles large data efficiently. 
  • 16 April 2026 SAP Business Technology Platform helps address the challenge of integrating, extending, and modernising SAP landscapes by offering a unified platform for integration, analytics, data management, and application development. For us, this means simpler system integration, better visibility through analytics, and faster innovation without disrupting our core SAP systems. 
  • 10 April 2026 By utilizing the SAP Integration Suite, we successfully automated the data flow between our core ERP and third-party tools. The biggest benefit is the dramatic reduction in manual IT maintenance, saving us countless hours each month. 
  • 16 March 2026 SAP Business Technology Platform helps us connect systems, manage data, and build extensions for SAP apps without changing the core system, keeping it stable. It speeds up development by allowing integration and new functions, ensuring easy modifications without risking the main setup. 
  • 12 February 2026  SAP technology primarily solves the problem of disconnected, inefficient, and siloed business processes within organizations. By acting as a "central nervous system," SAP integrates data across finance.         

And this is just a small sample of the reviews available on G2 – there are many more that speak about the benefits of SAP BTP not only for integration, but application development, automation and support for clean core development too.

Springing forward with new badges

Having secured our Spring 2026 badges from G2, what next for SAP BTP? Obviously, Summer 2026 badges are now in view, and with support from our fabulous customers we shall hope to achieve these next. But first for the SAP BTP team, along with others from SAP, will be a focus on our very own Sapphire 2026 events, due to take place in Orlando and Madrid next month. The SAP BTP team will be presenting at both of these prestigious events, so if you happen to be joining one or the other, please make sure to come and say hello to the team on the show floor. And if you’re unable to join either of these events in-person, then that’s no problem, instead please join us at the SAP Sapphire Virtual event.

 With that I’ll sign off for this time, but meanwhile don’t forget to go to the G2 website and read more reviews for the customer-viewpoint on SAP BTP!

Chris Grundy, Product Marketing, SAP BTP

entry.title: Triggering SAP CPI iflow through SAP Build Process Automation
entry.content:
This blog is for highlighting the basics of using SAP Build Process Automation and how it can be leveraged in various business scenarios. For demo purpose, I have created SAP CPI iflow which gets triggered by using a Automation job created in SAP BPA.

 

  1. Create CPI iflow test_iflow as below to capture data from Northwind opensource odata services with HTTPs sender adapter- 

 2. Activate the CPI iflow - 

 3. Create Destination in SAP BTP account using oauth credentials (client-id, client-secret created in BTP account) 

 

4. Add the created Destination in SAP Build->Control_Tower as below-  

 

 5. Create ‘Project’ ztestingprj_iflow under Connectors->Actions as below-  

 

 

 6.

a.  Create ‘Action’ zjoule_action under project ‘ztestingprj_iflow’ with URL [/http/ztest_joule] from endpoint generated from CPI iflow ‘test_iflow’. 

b. In the ‘Output’ tab, we will capture only some part of the response payload in the Body as the result.

c. In Destination, select the destination which was created in BTP 

d. Click Test. The Action triggers CPI iflow ‘test_iflow’ generating response [200 OK] with payload generated by CPI iflow.  

 

 7. Create ‘Process Automation’ project zProcess_prj under ‘Lobby’  

 

8. In Process Automation project created above; create Automation Artifact zauto1_sampleprocess as below- 

 

 9. In Automation Artifact created above, drag and drop the Action created in Step 6. 

 

 10. In ‘Project Properties’ create Environment Variable of type ‘destination’ as below and Save the  Automation Artifact- 

 11. To run the Automation in Desktop Agent create Automation Launcher zAutoLaunch1 as below- 

 

 12. In Automation Launcher created above drag and drop the Automation Artifact created in step 8. 

 13. Save the project. Release the required version and Deploy in ‘Public’ environment as below- 

 

 14. During Deployment, select the Destination created in BTP (step 3) in the Environment variable created in step 10.  

Click Deploy. 

 

 15. After successful Deployment the deployed version could be seen in the path as below- 

 16. Download Desktop Agent from SAP Development Tools as below and install in your system- 

 

 17. In Desktop Agent, click Add Tenant and register your SAP BPA account as below- 

 18. For Automation, set the Desktop Agent in ‘Unattended Mode’ as below- 

 

 19. After successful Tenant registration in Desktop Agent; we can see its entry in Agent Management under Control Tower path as below- 

 20. In ‘Triggers’ tab, create a trigger as per required schedule- 

 

 21. We can observe automatic triggering of the CPI iflow with the response payload- 

 22. In Monitoring->Automation Jobs; we can see the entries as per scheduled timings of trigger- 

 23. On Activating Traces, we can see the Traces showing response payloads coming from CPI iflow-

entry.title: How to undeploy a Joule project in SAP Build environments
entry.content:
Step 1: Open the project and find the release version you have deployed in the environment.

Step 2: Click on Deploy and, select the enviroment and then click on Undeploy.

This step is important to be able to delete Projects and/or Actions.

If this tip was or is helpful, don't forget to leave a kudo.

Best regards,

Erick

entry.title: Demo Scenario: Clean Core Extensibility with ABAP Cloud and SAP Build
entry.content:
Co-Authors: @Sivakumar_Subramaniam , @ShivaniShankar 

A common feedback when talking about clean core extensibility is the absence of demo-ready content to visualize these guidelines that have evolved over time. This blog post attempts to take an extensibility scenario as example and showcase the equivalent scenario in a clean-core compliant world. Nevertheless, we cannot claim to be covering every possible flow in one post. The intent is to showcase one example covering a wide range. In the process, we walk through some of the decisions taken that go beyond technology and consider business aspects as well. A lot of technical details are omitted since this post is already quite long. Look forward to the discussions and feedback in the comments section to assess interest and demand for similar posts/more technical versions. 

Recommended Pre-read: 

While an effort is made to abstract technical complexity to a great extent, a reader still needs some basic understanding of the SAP ecosystem, how extensions work, why they exist and some background of clean core.

To maximize takeaways from this blog post, consider reading the clean core extensibility whitepaper before you start, particularly if "Clean Core" sounds unfamiliar. Due to the rich context, answers to why clean core etc available in the whitepaper already, we skip it here. Nevertheless, a lot of what this blog post covers would not make much sense without this background/context. 

Key Assumptions: 

1. We consider level-A of clean core extensibility since this is the highest standard. If one were to target level-B for example, the target architecture might look a little different with potential reuse of certain objects

2. For simplicity and to keep the focus on extensibility, we assume there is no fit-to-standard solution available for this scenario which may or may not be factually correct. In a real project, please consider fit-to-standard analysis and an analysis of your existing custom-developed artefacts before developing something new

Scenario Description : 
- Provide a capability to add custom gift cards when creating sales orders in SAP Cloud ERP Private (S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition)

- On applying the gift card, a discount is added to the sales order by an amount equal to the gift card amount

- On creation of a sales order, loyalty points need to get accrued against the membership of the business partner (from the sales order). For simplicity, assume only business partners of type "Customer"

- The loyalty program has tiers and based on the tier the customer is on, he/she/they accrue a percentage of the sales order value as points

- It shall be possible for a customer to request an upgrade to their loyalty program tier. In this case, approval from an admin is required before the change is active 

Scenario Refinement:  

Understanding/context: When working with sales orders, this company has 2 parallel reward programs. One, a gift card that can be applied on creation of the sales order for an immediate discount. Another, a loyalty program where one accrues points with each sales order. Potentially, for redemption (not in scope here) through different means at a later point in time

Tasks:

- Since gift cards need to be applied, there is also an unsaid need to be able to create and maintain these gift cards (by an admin). This means an app to create/maintain gift cards with appropriate fields

- To be able to apply the gift card when creating a sales order, an option is needed directly in the sales order application (or transaction)

- The applied gift card should impact the value of the sales order before creation (not post-processing) 

- Similarly, loyalty program tiers would need to be maintained (possibly as business configuration)

- The loyalty points are calculated based on the created sales order and don't have a dependency or need to update/modify the consistency of the sales order itself. This could be handled as a separate activity from the creation of the sales order

Solution Architecture (Classic Extensibility - non-Clean Core level-A):In the classic extensibility scenario which is non-clean core level-A, we use the following technology choices to build this - 

  1. Gift Card Maintenance - WebDynPro Application
  2. Capability to add gift card to sales order in VA01 (Transaction code for sales order creation) - User Exit
  3. Loyalty Management Application - Module Pool Program
  4. Tier upgrade - Classic workflow

 Solution Architecture (with ABAP Cloud and SAP Build - Clean Core)

In the clean-core level-A compliant extensibility scenario, we use the following technology choices to build this - 

  1. Gift Card Maintenance - Application developed using RAP (ABAP RESTful Application Programming Model) with on-stack developer extensibility. Yes! with real code written by a developer inside the S/4HANA system outside of key user tools and still the highest level of clean core! If this is against your understanding of clean core, read SAP's official whitepaper (section 3.2 at the time this blog post is written) linked in the pre-read section of this blog post!  
  2. Capability to add gift card to sales order in the standard sales order Fiori app  - Key User Extensibility
  3. Loyalty Management Application - Side-by-side extension using BTP ABAP Environment
  4. Maintenance of tiers of the loyalty program - Business Configuration in the BTP ABAP Environment (not shown in the demo except as value help)
  5. Tier upgrade - SAP Build Process Automation
  6. Communication between S/4HANA and BTP - Event Mesh (or Advanced Event Mesh)

Alternate options - The Loyalty Points Application could also have been developed side-by-side using CAP (Cloud Application Programming Model) with additional handling for tier maintenance. 

Decision Criteria (Technical and Business): 

  1. The use case around application of gift card has a very tight dependency on the sales order. The standard SAP sales order application needs to understand and bring up the gift cards for a value help, have the gift cards applied consistently across impacted business objects when the sales order is updated. All of this needs to happen as part of the sales order creation/update process in S/4HANA. This makes on-stack developer extensibility and key-user extensibility good candidates. 
  2. The loyalty points application has 2 factors to consider - Side-by-side v/s on-stack and CAP v/s ABAP Cloud
    • Side-by-Side v/s On-Stack:
      • Decision: Side-by-Side
      • In private cloud/on-premise, SAP ships a new major version every 2 years. By moving out of the core (S/4HANA) anything that can be moved logically without adverse performance/over-engineering, etc, the customer can benefit from always being on the latest release and getting the latest updates. This de-risks innovation from upgrade project timelines and upgrade strategy. 
      • Enforcement of clean core - With the side-by-side approach, clean core is enforced with syntax checks etc since ABAP Cloud is the only option on BTP ABAP Environment as opposed to governance based approaches on-stack which may need an ABAP Cloud package and processes to ensure a developer doesn't slip back to the familiar classic extensibility when cloud ready alternatives are available.
    • CAP v/s ABAP Cloud
      • Decision: Both options are valid. Here, ABAP Cloud due to developer skill set and business configuration handling know-how. Since each option has strengths and benefits, refer the BTP Developer's guide for details. To generalize any of the two as always the better option without case-to-case analysis and comparison may be an unfair/sub-optimal decision. 

General question on BTP ABAP Environment cost -
"The cost of BTP ABAP Environment is higher" - Apart from the smaller runtime size now available, which reduces costs, the cost needs to be spread out across applications being developed. One instance of the BTP ABAP Environment can be reused for multiple applications so long as the runtime/memory requirements are within available limits. Compare this to additional costs for CF Runtime (for example) for each deployed app with CAP and the costing at solution level at scale tends to be similar. This is not to say CAP is a bad option, just that when the decision is influenced by cost, how this cost is calculated decides quite a bit! When this becomes equivalent, the models can be compared on best fit for the use case on technical/architectural grounds.

Demo of functional scenario in both approaches along with ATC findings across levels - B, C, D on the package where classic extensibility was used

The following demo showcases the scenario with classic extensibility as well as with Clean Core Level-A. It also touches upon ATC checks on the package containing the classic extension. To understand how ATC (ABAP Test Cockpit) helps categorize the findings, refer section 3.3.4(at the time of writing) of the clean core extensibility whitepaper (in the pre-read section)

 

Call to Action: Comment below what you would wish was better covered in the post and what key takeaways you had! Your inputs help us understand and validate where such blog posts can add more value

entry.title: Enhancing Enterprise Applications: AI-powered User Experience with SAP CAP AI Plugin
entry.content:
In today’s world, users expect their applications to smartly reduce their manual effort and help them complete their tasks quickly.

And enterprise applications are no exception. Imagine looking at a huge, blank form that you must fill out to complete a mundane but mandatory task at the office. You can practically feel the energy drain out of your body as the form stares back at you. Suddenly, a coffee break looks enticing 🙂

But what if your application could generate recommendations based on the data available in the system, and dramatically cut your effort to just eyeballing the correctness of most of the fields, only requiring you to fill in a few?

While this may be on every user’s wish list, implementing these AI-driven smart features in existing (or even new) applications can require a lot of development effort.

At SAP Cloud Application Programming (CAP), we believe in reducing boilerplate code and letting developers focus on intent. Hence, in the April release of CAP, we have released the SAP CAP AI plugin for node.js. (Watch this space for the Java version, which will be released soon).

This plugin will enable existing and new CAP applications to use recommendations generated by the RPT-1 Generative AI model provided by SAP AI Core. The recommendations will then be pre-filled in the UI of the application. All this is out-of-the-box, without writing a single line of code.

The CAP AI plugin does three main things:

  1. Simplifies AI Core configuration
  2. Provide recommendations on the UI coming from RPT-1
  3. Inherits the best of CAP

Simplifying AI Core configuration –

Admin tasks such as creating a new SAP AI Core resource group per tenant and creating an RPT-1 deployment are automated using the “AICore” service created by the plugin.

Recommendations in the UI application –

Any entity with a value help (@Common.ValueList on the property or @CDS.odata.valuelist on the association target) will be used by the plugin to show the recommendations.

 

entity Books {
  key ID : Integer;
  title  : String(111);
  descr  : String(1111);
  genre : Association to one Genres;
  status : Association to one Status;
}
annotate Genres with @cds.odata.valuelist;
annotate Books with {
    status @Common.ValueList : {
        CollectionPath : 'Status',
        Parameters: [
            {
                $Type: 'Common.ValueListParameterInOut'
                ValueListProperty : 'code',
                LocalDataProperty : status_code
            }
        ]
    }
}

 

 

The best of CAP

All the battle-tested enterprise readiness of CAP is inherently part of the plugin. It also supports multi-tenancy, and local testing without an SAP AI Core instance.

You can read more about the plugin here - https://github.com/cap-js/ai

We are excited to have you try out this plugin.  If you have any feature requests or suggestions, please feel free to reach out to us.

Till then, happy coding!

entry.title: Something Exciting is on the Horizon: Your Sapphire 2026 Session Guide
entry.content:
If you have not already, check out Mark Wright's blog post on What to Expect from AI Agents and Agentic Development — it sets the stage perfectly for what is coming.

Sapphire 2026  is shaping up to be one of the most exciting yet. This year, we will be unveiling a wave of new AI features that our team has been developing behind the scenes. We cannot wait to share them with you.

Whether you are just getting started with SAP Build and Joule Studio or you are already an expert, there is something here for you. Our sessions are designed to meet you where you are — giving you the insights, demos, and hands-on learning to take your skills to the next level.

Join us at Sapphire 2026 — register for the format that works best for you:

  • SAP Sapphire & ASUG Annual Conference Orlando
  • SAP Sapphire Virtual
  • SAP Sapphire Madrid

 

If you are planning to visit SAP Sapphire in person, stop by our live event demo booth to meet the team and see innovation in action. Discover how SAP Build helps teams create AI-powered solutions—from extensions to workflows and agentic apps—so you can deliver faster with more agility and less complexity. Do not miss our Agent Lab experience, where you can take part in a hands-on session and see just how easy it is to build AI agents that streamline and simplify your daily work:

  • Orlando station (BTP2301) and Agent Lab Experience Hub (EXP2788)
  • Madrid station (BTP1408) and Agent Lab Experience Hub (EXP1537)

 

To help you plan your agenda, we have also curated a selection of must-attend sessions. These highlight the latest AI capabilities for agent-based applications and workflow development, bringing them to life through real-world examples.

One session, however, stands out as essential: “The Enterprise Path to Agentic AI” (BTP1454). Presented by Michael Gall, Sid Misra, and Corey Swenson, this session explores how Joule Studio enables the creation of AI solutions ranging from simple extensions to fully agentic applications. It also highlights SAP’s strategy for delivering an enterprise-grade, intent-to-execution platform tailored for SAP customers. Attendees will see how the Joule solution provides a unified, guided experience — leading developers from initial intent through blueprinting and agent specification to testing and deployment.
